abstract: Choose a font family for Apple News Format that’s supported in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS.
overview:
## Overview

Fonts determine the look of text in your article. Within font families, the properties `fontWeight`, `fontWidth`, and `fontStyle` provide variations on the font for bolder or lighter, condensed or expanded, and regular or italicized text. To specify a font variant in Apple News Format, you can also use an explicit font variant’s PostScript name (listed in parentheses) in a `fontName` property without style modifiers (see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/TextStyle]). For information about using fonts in Apple News Format, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/defining-and-applying-text-styles].

### Choose a Font Family

The following table lists font families for Apple News Format that are supported in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS.

### Apply the Fonts to Your Article

The following example code shows how to apply the Arial font family to your article.

items.description: Define and apply custom, default, and inline text styles, or use HTML tags or Markdown syntax to style your text.
items.name: Defining and Applying Text Styles

items.description: The object for defining the text style, such as font family, size, and color, that you can apply to ranges of text.
items.name: TextStyle

items.description: The object for defining the style for a text component, including spacing, alignment, and drop caps.
items.name: ComponentTextStyle

items.description: The object for defining the drop cap text style to use in the first paragraph in a text component.
items.name: DropCapStyle

items.description: The object for defining the style for bulleted or numbered lists in an article.
items.name: ListItemStyle

items.description: The object for applying text styling when not using HTML or Markdown formatting.
items.name: InlineTextStyle

title: Applying Apple News Format Fonts
